diagnostic_explanation: Both reviewers correctly keep the claim unsupported, but they justify it differently. On citations, option_1 cites only evidence-004 even though its rationale also relies on recovery and planning-issue points that correspond to evidence-002 and evidence-003; option_2 cites nothing despite explicitly invoking those evidentiary points. Because the claim 'label_team_unreliable' is best assessed against the planning explanation, later recovery, and current team assessment together, option_2's citation behavior is still inadequate but diagnostically closer to the actual evidentiary basis than option_1's single cited item, so option_2 is slightly more adequate. On reasons, both share central_proposition_unsupported, while option_1 uses counterexample_preserved and option_2 uses counterexample_ignored; these are different framings of the same basic problem that contrary evidence remains in the record and was not reconciled by the claim, so the reason sets are complementary rather than materially conflicting. Confidence does not differ because both are marked high and nothing in the item clearly distinguishes calibration between them.
